zsh.h(3) Library Functions Manual zsh.h(3)

NAME

zsh.h - Zsh shell completion generator.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t completions_generate_zsh (FILE *output)
Generate zsh completion script.

Detailed Description

Zsh shell completion generator.

Definition in file zsh.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t completions_generate_zsh (FILE * output)

Generate zsh completion script.

Definition at line 200 of file zsh.c.

200                                                          {
201   if (!output) {
202     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Output stream cannot be NULL");
203   }
204 
205   /* Load binary-level and discovery options for binary-level completion.
206    * Binary-level completion (options before mode name like --color-mode) uses
207    * _arguments with -n -S flags to establish proper zsh completion context.
208    * Without _arguments, completion functions fail with "can only be called from
209    * completion function" error, causing zsh to display "corrections (errors: N)"
210    * messages. The -n flag disables defaults and -S disables short option
211    * processing, allowing safe context establishment. */
212   size_t binary_count = 0;
213   const option_descriptor_t *binary_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY, true, &binary_count);
214 
215   size_t discovery_count = 0;
216   const option_descriptor_t *discovery_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY, false, &discovery_count);
217 
218   fprintf(output, "compdef _ascii_chat ascii-chat\n"
219                   "compdef _ascii_chat 'build/bin/ascii-chat'\n"
220                   "# Zsh completion script for ascii-chat\n"
221                   "# Generated from options registry - DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY\n"
222                   "\n"
223                   "\n"
224                   "# Configure completion for ascii-chat\n"
225                   "# Use only _complete (no corrections or approximate matching)\n"
226                   "zstyle ':completion:*:ascii-chat:*' completer _complete\n"
227                   "\n"
228                   "# Device index completion helpers\n"
229                   "_ascii_chat_webcam_indices() {\n"
230                   "  local -a indices\n"
231                   "  local output\n"
232                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-webcams 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
233                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
234                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
235                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
236                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
237                   "  _describe 'webcam' indices\n"
238                   "}\n"
239                   "\n"
240                   "_ascii_chat_microphone_indices() {\n"
241                   "  local -a indices\n"
242                   "  local output\n"
243                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-microphones 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
244                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
245                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
246                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
247                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
248                   "  _describe 'microphone' indices\n"
249                   "}\n"
250                   "\n"
251                   "_ascii_chat_speakers_indices() {\n"
252                   "  local -a indices\n"
253                   "  local output\n"
254                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-speakers 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
255                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
256                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
257                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
258                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
259                   "  _describe 'speakers' indices\n"
260                   "}\n"
261                   "\n"
262                   "_ascii_chat_binary() {\n"
263                   "  local -a binary_opts=(\n");
264 
265   /* Add discovery options to binary-level completion */
266   if (discovery_opts) {
267     for (size_t i = 0; i < discovery_count; i++) {
268       fprintf(output, "    '--%s:", discovery_opts[i].long_name);
269       zsh_escape_desc(output, discovery_opts[i].help_text);
270       fprintf(output, "'\n");
271     }
272   }
273 
274   fprintf(output, "  )\n"
275                   "  _values 'option' \"${binary_opts[@]}\"\n"
276                   "}\n"
277                   "\n"
278                   "_ascii_chat_binary_grouped() {\n");
279 
280   /* Write binary-level options in grouped format */
281   if (binary_opts) {
282     zsh_write_value_cases(output, binary_opts, binary_count);
283     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, binary_opts, binary_count, "binary");
284   }
285 
286   fprintf(output, "}\n"
287                   "\n"
288                   "_ascii_chat() {\n"
289                   "  if [[ ${words[2]} == -* ]]; then\n"
290                   "    # Binary-level options: show both binary and discovery mode options\n"
291                   "    # (since discovery is the default mode that runs at binary level)\n"
292                   "    _ascii_chat_binary_grouped\n"
293                   "    _ascii_chat_discovery\n"
294                   "    return\n"
295                   "  fi\n"
296                   "\n"
297                   "  # Mode routing: check words[2] to determine which mode function to call\n"
298                   "  case $words[2] in\n"
299                   "    # Server-like modes\n"
300                   "    server)\n"
301                   "      _ascii_chat_server\n"
302                   "      return\n"
303                   "      ;;\n"
304                   "    discovery-service)\n"
305                   "      _ascii_chat_discovery_service\n"
306                   "      return\n"
307                   "      ;;\n"
308                   "\n"
309                   "    # Client-like modes\n"
310                   "    client)\n"
311                   "      _ascii_chat_client\n"
312                   "      return\n"
313                   "      ;;\n"
314                   "    mirror)\n"
315                   "      _ascii_chat_mirror\n"
316                   "      return\n"
317                   "      ;;\n"
318                   "    # Default/unknown input - show mode selection only if truly empty, otherwise discovery\n"
319                   "    *)\n"
320                   "      # If words[2] is empty AND we're at position 2, show mode selection\n"
321                   "      # Otherwise (even for partial input like 'disc'), use discovery mode (the default)\n"
322                   "      if [[ -z \"${words[2]}\" && $CURRENT -eq 2 ]]; then\n"
323                   "        # Completing the mode name itself - show available modes\n"
324                   "        local -a server_modes client_modes\n"
325                   "        server_modes=(\n");
326 
327   /* Generate server-like modes from registry */
328   size_t mode_server_count = 0;
329   const mode_descriptor_t *mode_server_descs = get_modes_by_group("server-like", &mode_server_count);
330   if (mode_server_descs) {
331     for (size_t i = 0; i < mode_server_count; i++) {
332       fprintf(output, "          '%s:%s'\n", mode_server_descs[i].name, mode_server_descs[i].description);
333     }
334     SAFE_FREE(mode_server_descs);
335   }
336 
337   fprintf(output, "        )\n"
338                   "        client_modes=(\n");
339 
340   /* Generate client-like modes from registry (exclude discovery since it's the default mode) */
341   size_t mode_client_count = 0;
342   const mode_descriptor_t *mode_client_descs = get_modes_by_group("client-like", &mode_client_count);
343   if (mode_client_descs) {
344     for (size_t i = 0; i < mode_client_count; i++) {
345       /* Skip discovery mode - it's the default when no mode is specified */
346       if (strcmp(mode_client_descs[i].name, "discovery") == 0) {
347         continue;
348       }
349       fprintf(output, "          '%s:%s'\n", mode_client_descs[i].name, mode_client_descs[i].description);
350     }
351     SAFE_FREE(mode_client_descs);
352   }
353 
354   fprintf(output, "        )\n"
355                   "        _describe -t server-modes 'server-like modes' server_modes\n"
356                   "        _describe -t client-modes 'client-like modes' client_modes\n"
357                   "      else\n"
358                   "        # Default mode (discovery) - complete its options\n"
359                   "        _ascii_chat_discovery\n"
360                   "      fi\n"
361                   "      ;;\n"
362                   "  esac\n"
363                   "}\n"
364                   "\n"
365                   "# Server-like modes: handle incoming connections and stream management\n"
366                   "_ascii_chat_server() {\n");
367 
368   /* Server options - grouped by category */
369   size_t server_count = 0;
370   const option_descriptor_t *server_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_SERVER, false, &server_count);
371 
372   if (server_opts) {
373     zsh_write_value_cases(output, server_opts, server_count);
374     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, server_opts, server_count, "server");
375     SAFE_FREE(server_opts);
376   }
377 
378   fprintf(output, "}\n"
379                   "\n"
380                   "_ascii_chat_discovery_service() {\n");
381 
382   /* Discovery-service options - grouped by category */
383   size_t discovery_svc_count = 0;
384   const option_descriptor_t *discovery_svc_opts =
385       options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, false, &discovery_svc_count);
386 
387   if (discovery_svc_opts) {
388     zsh_write_value_cases(output, discovery_svc_opts, discovery_svc_count);
389     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, discovery_svc_opts, discovery_svc_count, "discovery_service");
390     SAFE_FREE(discovery_svc_opts);
391   }
392 
393   fprintf(output, "}\n"
394                   "\n"
395                   "# Client-like modes: connect to servers or render local media\n"
396                   "_ascii_chat_client() {\n");
397 
398   /* Client options - grouped by category */
399   size_t client_count = 0;
400   const option_descriptor_t *client_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_CLIENT, false, &client_count);
401 
402   if (client_opts) {
403     zsh_write_value_cases(output, client_opts, client_count);
404     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, client_opts, client_count, "client");
405     SAFE_FREE(client_opts);
406   }
407 
408   fprintf(output, "}\n"
409                   "\n"
410                   "_ascii_chat_mirror() {\n");
411 
412   /* Mirror options - grouped by category */
413   size_t mirror_count = 0;
414   const option_descriptor_t *mirror_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_MIRROR, false, &mirror_count);
415 
416   if (mirror_opts) {
417     zsh_write_value_cases(output, mirror_opts, mirror_count);
418     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, mirror_opts, mirror_count, "mirror");
419     SAFE_FREE(mirror_opts);
420   }
421 
422   fprintf(output, "}\n"
423                   "\n"
424                   "# Discovery mode: find sessions via discovery service (default mode when no mode specified)\n"
425                   "_ascii_chat_discovery() {\n");
426 
427   /* Discovery options - grouped by category (reuse already-loaded options) */
428   if (discovery_opts) {
429     zsh_write_value_cases(output, discovery_opts, discovery_count);
430     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, discovery_opts, discovery_count, "discovery");
431   }
432 
433   fprintf(output, "}\n"
434                   "\n"
435                   "_ascii_chat \"$@\"\n");
436 
437   SAFE_FREE(binary_opts);
438   SAFE_FREE(discovery_opts);
439 
440   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
441 }










Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.2
    ascii-chat
